body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 73/2026
Made under: Offence Act
Published in: No. 8 – B.C. Regs. 62 to 76, May 5, 2026
Deposit date: April 30, 2026

B.C. Reg. 73/2026, deposited April 30, 2026, under theOFFENCE ACT[section 132]. Order in Council 169/2026, approved and ordered April 30, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with
         the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that the Violation Ticket
         Administration and Fines Regulation, B.C. Reg. 89/97, is amended as set out in the
         attached Schedule. — N. SHARMA,Attorney General and Deputy Premier; S. CHANDRA HERBERT,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. 1	Schedule 1 of the Violation Ticket Administration and Fines Regulation, B.C. Reg. 89/97,
         is amended in column 2 of item 1A by adding the following paragraph: (m.01)Safe Access to Places of Public Worship Act. 2	Schedule 2 is amended by adding the following as indicated: Safe Access to Places of Public Worship Act Gathering that impedes access or egress Gathering that disrupts activities Gathering that engages in interference Gathering that intimidates or causes concern Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: New violation tickets for blocking access to places of worship in BC
minutes_briefs.summary: British Columbia has added the Safe Access to Places of Public Worship Act to the list of laws that can be enforced through violation tickets (a type of ticket that lets police or bylaw officers issue fines without going to court). This means that gatherings that block people from entering or leaving a place of worship, disrupt activities, interfere with services, or intimidate people can now result in a ticket and fine.
